name: Rainmaker
description: 16-tap stereo spectral rhythm delay and 64-tap comb resonator. Each tap: level, pan, 2nd-order filter, granular pitch shift, detune. 16 groove patterns and comb modes (Fibonacci, Karplus, Uniform) enable modal synthesis.

controls.name: DELAY FEEDBACK
controls.description: Amount of selected tap fed back to input. Passes through tone filter and pitch shifter before returning.

controls.name: PITCH SHIFT
controls.description: Global pitch shift added to all 16 delay taps and to the feedback path. Dead zone at 12 o'clock.

controls.name: FEEDBACK TONE
controls.description: 1st-order tone control in the feedback loop. CCW = lowpass · CW = highpass.

controls.name: COMB SIZE
controls.description: Delay time of the 64th (longest) tap. Defines the comb's fundamental frequency when used for Karplus-Strong resonance. Selectable source: knob, clock + DIV, or 1V/OCT.

controls.name: COMB FEEDBACK
controls.description: Amount of tap-64 signal fed back to input for resonating comb effect. High values produce sustained Karplus-Strong strings.

controls.name: MOD A / MOD B
controls.description: Two assignable modulation knobs with jacks. Each scales CV sent into its MOD input and either offsets or overrides the chosen MOD destination. Destinations selected via MOD DEST menu.
controls.detail: MOD A normals to MOD B when B unpatched · 30+ destinations

controls.name: DRY / MIX (WET)
controls.description: Wet/dry balance at the main OUT L/R jacks. Analog mixer — not digital.

controls.name: TEMPO
controls.description: Tap-tempo button. Sets beat time from tap rhythm (3+ taps). Overridden by external CLK input.
controls.detail: Yellow LED flashes at beat time

controls.name: TRIGGER (big red)
controls.description: Fires the assigned trigger operation: randomize, freeze, reverse, ping, mute, bypass, shaped pulse, preset load, clear buffer, etc. Operation set via ROUTING+CONFIG.

controls.name: CLEAR
controls.description: Clears delay and comb memory — kills persistent echoes or overloud feedback.

controls.name: EDIT / TAP#
controls.description: Toggles between EDIT mode (global rhythm delay parameters) and TAP# mode (per-tap parameter editing). The 16 blue buttons switch roles accordingly.

controls.name: Rotary encoder
controls.description: Turn to adjust selected parameter value. Push for alternate behaviours: RAND execute, PING-PONG toggle, RAMP presets, preset save/load confirm.

controls.name: Global param buttons (RAND / TAP GRID / STACK / CLK TIME / CLK DIV / TIME MOD WAVE/LEVEL/RATE)
controls.description: Select global rhythm delay parameters for editing with the encoder. Includes randomization destination, tap grid/stacks, clock time/div, and time modulation LFO (wave, depth, rate).

controls.name: Per-tap buttons (QTY / SIZE / TAP# / SLIP / PITCH / TYPE / AMT / REVERSE+WET/DRY)
controls.description: Second button row selects grain quantity/size, feedback tap number, feedback slip, feedback pitch/detune, groove type, groove amount, and reverse/wet-dry.

controls.name: TAP EDIT buttons (MUTE / LEVEL / PAN / CUT / Q / TYPE / PITCH SHIFT)
controls.description: Red button row selects which per-tap parameter is displayed/edited across all 16 taps. PITCH SHIFT press-twice reveals DETUNE (<0.5 semitone).

controls.name: FX ON / MUTE (Delay)
controls.description: FX ON toggles delay effect bypass (red LED = active). MUTE mutes the delay input — continues to regenerate existing material.

controls.name: FX ON / MUTE (Comb)
controls.description: FX ON toggles comb bypass. MUTE mutes the comb input.

controls.name: Comb buttons (PATT+SLOPE / SIZE MOD WAVE/LEVEL/RATE / TAPS+FB / CLK SRC / CLK DIV / SIZE)
controls.description: Select comb parameters: tap pattern (Uniform/Fib/Flat/Karplus etc.), #taps, feedback configuration (Guitar/Sitar/Clarinet/Raw), clock source and division, and comb size modulation.

controls.name: Routing + Config / Meters + Gain / Save / Load
controls.description: Routing selects DLY→CMB, CMB→DLY, DLY+CMB, or split L-dly/R-cmb. Config sets input mode and trigger operation. Meters+Gain monitors 8 signal levels. Save/Load handles 128 presets.

inputs.name: IN R
inputs.description: Right audio input. Depending on input mode: stereo, summed to mono, or independent.
inputs.normalizedTo: IN L when in MONO L input mode

inputs.name: DELAY FEEDBACK CV
inputs.description: CV input for rhythm delay feedback (attenuator)

inputs.name: PITCH SHIFT CV
inputs.description: CV for global delay pitch shift. Accepts 1V/oct — sets feedback pitch. Square wave overrides TEMPO to set DELAY CLOCK TIME.

inputs.name: TRIG
inputs.description: Trigger input — fires assigned trigger operation (randomize, freeze, reverse, ping, etc.)

inputs.name: CLK (delay)
inputs.description: External clock for delay — feeds CLK TIME when CLK SRC is set to clock input

inputs.name: CLK (comb)
inputs.description: External clock for comb — sets comb size with DIV

inputs.name: 1V/OCT
inputs.description: Pitch CV for COMB SIZE — linear pitch control of comb fundamental (Karplus-style)

inputs.name: MOD A
inputs.description: CV for assignable MOD A destination

inputs.name: MOD B
inputs.description: CV for assignable MOD B destination

outputs.name: OUT L
outputs.description: Main left output — analog mix of input and processed signal

outputs.name: CLK
outputs.description: Square wave at rhythm delay beat time. Use to clock external modules in time with delay.

behaviors.name: 16-tap spectral delay
behaviors.description: 16 time-delayed taps, each with independent level, pan, 2nd-order filter (LP/BP/HP/None), granular pitch shift ±16 semitones, and fine detune. Taps sum into stereo output — a full granular/spectral delay in one module.

behaviors.name: 16 groove rhythms
behaviors.description: Straight, Swing, Hard Swing, Reverse Swing, Alternate Swing, Accelerando, Ritardando, Waltz, Half Swing, Roller Coaster, Quintuple, 3× Uniform Random, Early/Late Reflection. GROOVE AMT blends between Straight and selected — 0=even, 100=full groove.

behaviors.name: Per-tap pitch stacks
behaviors.trigger: TAP STACK parameter (panel silkscreen reads PILES; renamed to STACK in firmware v1.05+)
behaviors.description: Each tap can become a chord of multiple pitched copies at one time slot. Create four-note harmonized echoes or simulate a chord on every delay tap. Combine with pitch ramp for arpeggiated stacks.

behaviors.name: Reverse mode
behaviors.trigger: TRIG operation = Reverse or REVERSE+WET/DRY encoder
behaviors.description: Plays the delay buffer backwards through the tap structure. Combine with long delay times and pitch shift for spectral reverse-reverb trails.

behaviors.name: 64-tap comb resonator
behaviors.description: Separate 64-tap delay line summed for comb/reverberation effects. Active tap count adjustable 1–64. 16 tap patterns (Uniform, Flat Middle, Early Comb, Fibonacci, Flat Comb, Late Comb, Rev Fib, Ess, plus 8 randomized variants).

behaviors.name: Karplus-Strong strings
behaviors.description: Comb section emulates plucked strings. Short input bursts excite the comb; 1V/OCT input tracks pitch. Feedback config changes timbre: Guitar (exponential), Sitar (modulated bridge), Clarinet (rectified), Raw (no nonlinearity).

behaviors.name: Four routing modes
behaviors.description: DLY→CMB: delay feeds comb (serial). CMB→DLY: comb feeds delay. DLY+CMB: parallel sum. L:DLY R:CMB: stereo split — mono delay left, mono comb right.

behaviors.name: Trigger modes (15+)
behaviors.description: Freeze, Reverse, Randomize, Delay/Comb Mute, Delay/Comb Bypass, Ping Input, Shaped Pulse, Toggle Ping-Pong, Gated Freeze, Gated Reverse, Preset Load, Clear Buffer. Trigger jack assigned to one mode at a time.

behaviors.name: Randomization surgery
behaviors.description: Randomize only selected parameter set: groove times, tap mutes, levels, pans, filter cutoffs, Qs, types, pitch shifts, detunes, all taps, all delay, all taps+delay, all comb, all delay+comb, all, or random-of-above. Surgical generative variation.

behaviors.name: Time modulation LFO
behaviors.description: Dedicated LFO modulates delay tap time. Waveforms: Sinusoid, Triangle, Vibrato 1, Vibrato 2, Down Chirp, Up Chirp, Random. LEVEL sets modulation amplitude · RATE sets period (Hz display).

behaviors.name: Pitch tap (tap#) feedback
behaviors.description: Selects which of the 16 taps feeds back to input. Different tap choice changes the rhythmic regeneration interval — short tap = fast flutter, long tap = slow looping echoes. SLIP offsets fractional timing.

behaviors.name: 128 factory presets
behaviors.description: 128 non-volatile slots. First 82 are factory (Krellverb, Karplus, Spiral, Chorus, Flanger, Symphony, 40-Tap String, AllTap, TechnoLoop, Resonator, etc.); 46 are user slots. MIDI SysEx export/import.

patchIdeas.name: Polyrhythmic 16-tap delay
patchIdeas.patch: Load preset 8 '16tap Beat'. External clock locks CLK TIME. GROOVE = Hard Swing shoves the 16 tap positions into a swung grid; per-tap pans spread them across the stereo field.
steps: Hold LOAD, turn encoder to preset 8 '16tap Beat', push encoder to load.
Press ROUTING+CONFIG, set routing to DLY only.
Press EDIT, then GROOVE TYPE button; turn encoder to 'Hard Swing'.
Press GROOVE AMT button; turn encoder to ~70 (strong swing).
Press TAP#, then PAN red button; set odd taps hard left, even taps hard right for L/R scatter.
Patch external clock (0–5V square) to Rainmaker CLK (delay) — TEMPO is overridden.
Patch synth/drums to IN L.
Patch OUT L to mixer L, OUT R to mixer R.
Set DRY/MIX to ~1 o'clock (mostly wet) so the groove is audible.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT L / OUT R — dry plus 16 swung, stereo-panned echoes at -5V to +5V, clock-locked to your external CLK.
patchIdeas.hear: Every hit scatters into 16 swung echoes that leap between the left and right speakers — a polyrhythmic drum fill from a single note.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Rainmaker:
* Preset: 16tap Beat · GROOVE: Hard Swing · AMT 70
* Routing: DLY only

Clock:

- Synth (Out) -> Rainmaker (IN L)
- Clock (Out) c> Rainmaker (CLK Delay)
- Rainmaker (OUT L) -> Output (Left)
- Rainmaker (OUT R) -> Output (Right)

patchIdeas.name: Karplus-Strong string synth
patchIdeas.patch: Comb-only routing plus 1V/OCT on COMB SIZE turns the 64-tap comb into a plucked string. A gated noise burst excites it; Guitar feedback config gives an exponential-averaging string timbre.
steps: Press ROUTING+CONFIG, set routing to CMB only.
In the Comb section, press TAPS+FB once then press again to reach FEEDBACK config; turn encoder to 'Guitar'.
Press CLK SRC; turn encoder to '1V/OCT' so COMB SIZE tracks pitch.
Turn COMB FEEDBACK knob to ~90% (long ringing decay, just under runaway).
Patch sequencer Gate to ENV Trig.
Patch Noise to VCA In, ENV Out to VCA CV — short bursts are the excite signal.
Patch VCA Out to Rainmaker IN L.
Patch sequencer Pitch (1V/oct) to Rainmaker 1V/OCT jack.
Patch Rainmaker OUT L and OUT R to mixer.
Set DRY/MIX full CW (fully wet — you want the string, not the click).
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT L / OUT R — pitched plucked-string tone at -5V to +5V, decay length set by COMB FEEDBACK, pitch tracks 1V/oct.
patchIdeas.hear: A bright, plucked steel-string that plays your sequencer. Higher feedback = longer sustain; swap Guitar → Sitar for a bent, rectified shimmer, Clarinet for a hollow square-wave tone, Raw for noisy overdrive.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Rainmaker:
* Routing: CMB only · Comb FB: 90% · Config: Guitar
* COMB CLK SRC: 1V/OCT

Sequencer:

Noise:

ENV:

VCA:

- Sequencer (Gate) g> ENV (Trig)
- Noise (Out) -> VCA (In)
- ENV (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- VCA (Out) -> Rainmaker (IN L)
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Rainmaker (1V/OCT)
- Rainmaker (OUT L) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Ascending pitch-shift cascade
patchIdeas.patch: Set each tap's PITCH SHIFT to a different semitone so one input hit triggers a 16-step ascending line through the echoes.
steps: Press EDIT, then GROOVE TYPE; turn encoder to 'Straight' for an even grid.
Turn DELAY FEEDBACK knob to ~9 o'clock (low — you want each cascade to die before the next hit).
Press TAP# (red EDIT/TAP# button). Press PITCH SHIFT red button.
Select tap 1 (blue button), turn encoder to 0 semis. Tap 2 = +1, Tap 3 = +2 … Tap 16 = +15 (per-tap range is -16 to +15).
Tip: push the encoder once to access ramp presets — 'Rising' sets all 16 taps to a ramp automatically.
Set each tap LEVEL equal (press LEVEL button, set all to ~24).
Patch source (pluck, vocal, drum) to IN L.
Patch OUT L and OUT R to mixer.
Set DRY/MIX to ~1 o'clock (mostly wet).
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT L / OUT R — single input hit sprays 16 echoes rising chromatically at -5V to +5V.
patchIdeas.hear: Play one note and hear a 16-step chromatic staircase climb away from you. Swap to +2 steps per tap (0, +2, +4 …) for a whole-tone ladder, or use the 'Rise/Fall' ramp preset for a pitch arch.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Rainmaker:
* Taps 1-16: pitch shift 0,+2,+4...+16
* GROOVE: Straight · DLY FB: low

- Synth (Out) -> Rainmaker (IN L)
- Rainmaker (OUT L) -> Output (Left)
- Rainmaker (OUT R) -> Output (Right)

patchIdeas.name: Spectral filter bank reverb
patchIdeas.patch: Tune each tap filter to a harmonic of the source and pan them across the stereo field — Rainmaker becomes a 16-band resonant filter bank.
steps: Press TAP#, then TYPE red button; set every tap TYPE to 'BP' (bandpass).
Press Q red button; set every tap Q to ~12 (high resonance, just below self-oscillation).
Press CUT red button; set taps to a harmonic series: tap 1 ~100Hz, tap 2 ~200Hz, tap 3 ~300Hz … tap 16 ~1.6kHz (use the display readout).
Press PITCH SHIFT, set all taps to 0 semitones (no transposition).
Press PAN; sweep pans evenly 7L → 7R across taps 1–16.
Press LEVEL, set all taps equal (~24).
Patch voice/pad to IN L.
Patch OUT L / OUT R to mixer.
Set DRY/MIX to 12 o'clock for a resonant halo over the dry source.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT L / OUT R — source plus 16 resonant bandpass echoes tuned to a harmonic series, spread across stereo.
patchIdeas.hear: Your voice gains a choral, organ-like resonant body — each harmonic rings out from a different point in the stereo field. Lower the tap cutoffs for a darker formant; raise Q for near-self-oscillating drones.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Rainmaker:
* Tap filters: BP · cutoffs harmonic series
* Tap Q: high · Tap pans: spread L↔R

- Voice (Out) -> Rainmaker (IN L)
- Rainmaker (OUT L) -> Output (Left)
- Rainmaker (OUT R) -> Output (Right)

patchIdeas.name: Generative random reflections
patchIdeas.patch: A slow clock into TRIG fires the Randomizer every few bars, scrambling only tap pitches — the reharmonization rewrites itself under an ambient bed.
steps: Press ROUTING+CONFIG three times to reach TRIGGER MODE; turn encoder to 'Randomize'.
Press EDIT, then RAND button; turn encoder to destination 'Tap Pitch Shifts' (only pitches get randomized).
Turn DELAY FEEDBACK to ~11 o'clock for a tail that carries between randomizations.
Press EDIT, then GROOVE TYPE; turn encoder to 'Early Reflection' (sparse, reverb-like).
Patch your ambient source (pad, field recording) to IN L.
Patch a clock divider set to every 4 bars (≥+1V pulse) to Rainmaker TRIG.
Patch OUT L / OUT R to mixer.
Set DRY/MIX to ~1 o'clock so the wet bed dominates.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT L / OUT R — ambient source plus a reflection cloud whose pitches re-scramble every 4 bars.
patchIdeas.hear: A slowly reharmonizing halo under your pad — every four bars the echoes land on a new set of intervals. Switch the rand destination to 'All delay' for a more dramatic shuffle.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Rainmaker:
* TRIG op: Randomize · Dest: Tap Pitch Shifts

Clock Div:

- Ambient (Out) -> Rainmaker (IN L)
- Clock Div (Out) t> Rainmaker (TRIG)
- Rainmaker (OUT L) -> Output (Left)
- Rainmaker (OUT R) -> Output (Right)

patchIdeas.name: Dual delay + Karplus split
patchIdeas.patch: Routing 'L:DLY R:CMB' runs the rhythm delay on the left input and the Karplus comb on the right — two effects from one module.
steps: Press ROUTING+CONFIG; turn encoder to 'L:DLY R:CMB' (left input → delay → OUT L; right input → comb → OUT R).
In the Comb section press TAPS+FB twice and set FEEDBACK config to 'Guitar' (Karplus).
Press CLK SRC; set to '1V/OCT'.
Turn COMB FEEDBACK to ~85% for a singing string.
For the delay side, press EDIT then GROOVE TYPE; pick 'Swing'. Turn DELAY FEEDBACK to ~11 o'clock.
Patch drum loop to IN L (delay side).
Patch excite noise/pluck to IN R (comb side).
Patch sequencer Pitch (1V/oct) to Rainmaker 1V/OCT.
Patch OUT L and OUT R to mixer (stereo split).
Set DRY/MIX to 12 o'clock.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT L — drum loop + rhythmic delay. OUT R — pitched Karplus string. Each at -5V to +5V.
patchIdeas.hear: Left ear gets a swinging rhythmic echo of the drums; right ear gets a tracked Karplus melody — the two effects run in parallel out of one module.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Rainmaker:
* Routing: L:DLY R:CMB
* Comb: Karplus config

Sequencer:

- Drums (Out) -> Rainmaker (IN L)
- Excite (Out) -> Rainmaker (IN R)
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Rainmaker (1V/OCT)
- Rainmaker (OUT L) -> Output (Left)
- Rainmaker (OUT R) -> Output (Right)

patchIdeas.name: Freeze-and-pitch glitch
patchIdeas.patch: TRIG set to Freeze locks the delay buffer; a slow LFO into PITCH SHIFT CV shifts all 16 taps ±2 octaves while you play over the melting loop.
steps: Press ROUTING+CONFIG three times to TRIGGER MODE; turn encoder to 'Freeze'.
Turn DELAY FEEDBACK to ~2 o'clock (high — the frozen loop regenerates).
Press EDIT, GROOVE TYPE; choose 'Straight'.
Patch source to IN L and start playing.
Tap Rainmaker's big red TRIGGER button (or send a manual gate to TRIG) on the moment you want to freeze.
Patch a slow bipolar LFO (±5V, 0.05–0.3 Hz) to PITCH SHIFT CV — the global pitch shift sweeps all 16 taps ±2 octaves.
Patch OUT L / OUT R to mixer.
Set DRY/MIX full CW for all wet so only the melting buffer is heard.
Tap TRIGGER again to unfreeze and grab a new slice.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT L / OUT R — a frozen delay buffer whose pitch sweeps up and down as the LFO runs, at -5V to +5V.
patchIdeas.hear: The moment you hit TRIGGER, the delay stops listening and starts looping — and the LFO drags the pitch of the loop down into subs and up into shimmer. Keep playing dry over the top for a slowly dissolving ghost.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Rainmaker:
* TRIG op: Freeze · DLY FB: high

LFO:

Manual Gate:

- Source (Out) -> Rainmaker (IN L)
- Manual Gate (Out) t> Rainmaker (TRIG) // freeze
- LFO (Out) >> Rainmaker (PITCH SHIFT CV)
- Rainmaker (OUT L) -> Output (Left)
- Rainmaker (OUT R) -> Output (Right)

patchIdeas.name: Reverse reverb tail
patchIdeas.patch: TRIG set to 'Gated Reverse' plays the delay line backwards while the gate is high — each drum hit leaves a reverse-swell into the next.
steps: Press ROUTING+CONFIG three times to TRIGGER MODE; turn encoder to 'Gated Reverse'.
Press EDIT, GROOVE TYPE; choose 'Early Reflection' (sparse, reverb-like spread).
Turn DELAY FEEDBACK to ~12 o'clock (several audible laps).
Press EDIT, REVERSE+WET/DRY — make sure WET/DRY (internal delay mix) is ~12 o'clock.
Patch drum to IN L.
Patch the drum's trigger output (or a gate from its envelope) to Rainmaker TRIG. Gate HIGH = reverse, LOW = forward.
Patch OUT L / OUT R to mixer.
Set global DRY/MIX to ~1 o'clock.
Play the drum pattern — each hit leaves a reverse-swell trail into the next.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT L / OUT R — drum with a reverse-swell tail that leads into each next hit, at -5V to +5V.
patchIdeas.hear: Every drum hit blooms backwards before the next one lands — like a shoegaze reverse reverb but rhythmic, sitting inside the 16-tap reflection grid.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Rainmaker:
* TRIG op: Gated Reverse
* GROOVE: Early Reflection · FB: 50%

- Drum (Out) -> Rainmaker (IN L)
- Drum Trig (Out) g> Rainmaker (TRIG)
- Rainmaker (OUT L) -> Output (Left)
- Rainmaker (OUT R) -> Output (Right)

patchIdeas.name: Stacked chord generator
patchIdeas.patch: STACK = 4 per tap with per-stack pitches 0 / +4 / +7 / +12 turns every delay tap into a major-7 chord — one note in, chordal cascade out.
steps: Press EDIT, then TAP STACK button (labelled PILES on panel, renamed to STACK in v1.05 firmware).
Turn encoder to set STACK = 4 across taps (or press to configure stacks individually).
Set the four stack pitch offsets to 0, +4, +7, +12 semitones (major 7 chord).
Press EDIT, GROOVE TYPE; choose 'Straight'.
Turn DELAY FEEDBACK to ~10 o'clock (a couple of chord cascades before decay).
Patch a mono VCO/voice to IN L.
Patch OUT L / OUT R to mixer.
Set DRY/MIX to ~1 o'clock.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT L / OUT R — single mono note sprays a maj7 chord at every delay tap, at -5V to +5V.
patchIdeas.hear: Play one note, hear a glossy major-7 chord cascade away from you, tap after tap. Swap the stack pitches to 0 / +3 / +7 / +10 for a minor-7 voicing, or 0 / +5 / +7 for parallel fifths.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Rainmaker:
* STACK: 4 · per-stack pitch: 0,+4,+7,+12
* GROOVE: Straight

- VCO (Out) -> Rainmaker (IN L)
- Rainmaker (OUT L) -> Output (Left)
- Rainmaker (OUT R) -> Output (Right)

patchIdeas.name: Tempo-locked modulated resonator
patchIdeas.patch: Parallel DLY+CMB routing plus a slow TIME MOD LFO warps delay timing around the grid; Rainmaker's CLK out keeps the rest of the patch locked in time.
steps: Press ROUTING+CONFIG; turn encoder to 'DLY+CMB' (parallel sum).
Press EDIT, GROOVE TYPE; choose 'Roller Coaster'. Press GROOVE AMT; set to ~50.
Press EDIT, TIME MOD WAVE; turn encoder to 'Triangle'. Press TIME MOD LEVEL to ~5; press TIME MOD RATE to a slow setting (~0.1 Hz).
In the Comb section, press TAPS+FB; set #taps to 32. Turn COMB FEEDBACK to ~40% for modal body.
Tap TEMPO button three times to set beat time — yellow LED flashes at beat.
Patch percussion to IN L.
Patch Rainmaker's CLK output to an external ENV's Trig input — external voice is now locked to Rainmaker's delay beat.
Patch OUT L / OUT R to mixer.
Set DRY/MIX to 12 o'clock.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT L / OUT R — delayed percussion fused with comb modal resonance, at -5V to +5V. CLK out at 0–5V square locks external gear to the delay beat.
patchIdeas.hear: A rhythmic percussion echo that shifts around the grid like a roller-coaster while the comb adds a singing modal body underneath — every external module hanging off Rainmaker's CLK stays in lockstep.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Rainmaker:
* Routing: DLY+CMB · TIME MOD: Tri · slow
* GROOVE: Roller Coaster · AMT 50

- Perc (Out) -> Rainmaker (IN L)
- Rainmaker (CLK) c> ENV (Trig) // tempo lock
- Rainmaker (OUT L) -> Output (Left)
- Rainmaker (OUT R) -> Output (Right)
